verses.1: Wisdom has built her house, She has carved out her seven pillars;
verses.2: She has prepared her food, she has mixed her wine; She has also set her table;
verses.3: She has sent out her attendants, she calls out From the tops of the heights of the city:
verses.4: “Whoever is naive, let him turn in here!” To him who lacks understanding she says,
verses.5: “Come, eat of my food And drink of the wine I have mixed.
verses.6: “Abandon your foolishness and live, And proceed in the way of understanding.”
verses.7: One who corrects a scoffer gets dishonor for himself, And one who rebukes a wicked person gets insults for himself.
verses.8: Do not rebuke a scoffer, or he will hate you; Rebuke a wise person and he will love you.
verses.9: Give instruction to a wise person and he will become still wiser; Teach a righteous person and he will increase his insight.
verses.10: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom, And the knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
verses.11: For by me your days will be multiplied, And years of life will be added to you.
verses.12: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself, And if you scoff, you alone will suffer from it.
verses.13: A woman of foolishness is boisterous, She has a lack of understanding and knows nothing.
verses.14: She sits at the doorway of her house, On a seat by the high places of the city,
verses.15: Calling to those who pass by, Who are going straight on their paths:
verses.16: “Whoever is naive, let him turn in here,” And to him who lacks understanding she says,
verses.17: “Stolen water is sweet; And bread eaten in secret is pleasant.”
verses.18: But he does not know that the dead are there, That her guests are in the depths of Sheol.
